message: Jake
Yuriko
Kristy
Serial Murder Case
Ginny
Since then,
it seems that she has been sincerely attending Gunter's place.
Really, that's great.
She has started receiving counseling since then.
For someone like her, who had been pretty mentally drained even if just temporarily, it was necessary to have a good listener who understands her troubles.
Ideally, that could have been Guy or me,
Boss.
Huh? Oh, sorry, I was lost in thought.
She's okay now. She met a good counselor.
I just hope she keeps recovering smoothly.
As for the rest,
it would be great if Guy could come back soon.
Shall we try sorting out what has happened so far?
Yeah, that's right. So much has happened in such a short time, it feels like my head might explode.
Sort out
System
The requests I have on hand
matters
about Meryl
about Guy
the article on BAM
Save
Exit the game
We can't just hang around the office forever.
Right, let’s do our best to resolve these requests! Now, Boss, please do your best today too!
Sounds good.
Now stop whining and just get going.
Currently, there are two unresolved requests. One is from Meryl looking for her daughter, and the
other is a request from Gloria to find Cindy.
Yeah, those are the only two we have right now.
Oh come on, that sulky expression doesn’t suit you, so please stop it.
Anyway, the priority for our office is to meet with Anna Skakki, the midwife who holds the key to Meryl's daughter.
That’s the problem, huh?
If this isn’t resolved, our office’s reputation will take a nosedive, so do your best.
But you know, it’s originally a tough request.
Shut up! Please take action before complaining. That’s this month’s goal.
As for the other search for Cindy,
what does the Boss think?
It’s likely she didn’t get abducted but rather went missing voluntarily, and Gloria probably knows where she is.
I think.
Wow, the Boss sounds like a detective.
Are you making fun of me?
No, I’m actually a bit impressed. Hehe☆
I actually think the same. If it were serious, I think we would have heard from her or Gloria would be panicking.
The lack of contact from Gloria might be because she dislikes me though.
That still sounds strange. Isn’t it true that she was pushed to request help by her supervisor?
Oh,
you're sharp.
Hehe, what can I do if I don’t know this much?
Anyway, let’s work hard to solve these requests!
I hope you
get better soon.
Yeah.
If Guy shows up and makes amends with you,
everything will surely be all right.
I hope so.
Is there something you're unhappy about?
I think the current situation originated from the article in BAM and the fact that we happened to get into a fight around that time.
Yeah, I think that's probably true.
That's such an unsatisfying thing to say. Is there anything else?
No, that’s probably good enough.
Probably.
Boss, what's bothering you?
I just wonder if that could really be the only reason for the drastic change.
That's what I'm thinking.
You just don't understand a woman's heart, do you?
A delicate woman's heart can crumble with just a little provocation.
Yeah.
Well, that's how it goes.
That's right,
Meryl is just a poor person. I mean it, Guy, come back quickly!
It feels that way.
We really have to protect her.
Meryl is our client. Personally, I find her to be an insufferable type, but once she's a client, that’s a different story.
You really hate her, huh?
Oh, Boss, are your ears bad? I said Meryl is a good person.
That's what I said.
Oh come on.
You can't just pout and throw compliments!
Because…
It's okay, clients are good people. As long as they pay their fees, any person is a good person.
But why has Meryl ignored her daughter until now? That's what I don't understand.
Apparently, she swore to herself not to search until she succeeded.
I can't believe that. What does she plan to do if her daughter grows up without knowing anything?
I warned her that her daughter might be living a happy life without knowing anything, but Meryl really wants to see her daughter for her own sake.
That's just super egotistical.
If I were in my daughter's position, I absolutely couldn't forgive that.
What do you think about that person named Meryl, Boss?
Umm.
Well, I don't really know, but I'm sure she's a lonely person.
What on earth is that supposed to mean?
She seems to have worries both in her personal and professional life, and I think she’s trying her best in her own way.
Huh.
Well, even though she's called a great actress, she's just a regular person.
Either way, we need to find your daughter, right?
Guy is currently traveling. If we believe the BAM article, a romantic getaway with the talent Cindy can’t be ruled out.
Don't say things like that with a straight face. It’s just a trip for Guy.
Really? That makes it sound like he's running away.
Not 'sounds like he's running away'—he actually is running away.
Ugh.
Then there's nothing we can do about it, right?
Do you know where Guy went or who he went with? Have you found out yet?
No, not at all. Maybe Eva knows where he’s headed, but for now, there’s no sign she'll tell us.
Then that’s no good, is it?
Geez, this isn't going well.
Let's figure out where Guy's going.
And take him back to san.
The article in BAM might be the root cause of all this.
Root cause of what?
The collapse of san.
You should refrain from saying things like that. It’s rude to her.
Oh, I see. I'm sorry,
san.
But,
this article seems like someone provided the scoop, doesn’t it? And it must be someone Guy wouldn’t complain about.
Maybe Guy himself is the one who provided it.
No way—really?
I don't want to think about that.
If we kidnapped Eva and interrogated her, wouldn’t she just confess easily?
We shouldn't think of such extreme things.
After all, she seems like she knows a lot. That frustrates me.
And what good does that do?
Oh.
If only Eva would just tell us everything, it could solve some things.
Could it be that Eva and Guy are close?
